pipeline_reconciliation_note.issue: Two valid but distinct orderings exist across this package: Meridian_Master.json's generation_pipeline.generation_order sequences by DISTRICT/REGION (which region's foundational data must exist before another's). The brief for this file requests a sequence by CONTENT TYPE (roads, then buildings, then gameplay, etc.). These are not contradictory — they operate at different scopes — but presenting only one without reconciling the other would leave the Builder with two unrelated 'the order is X' claims.
pipeline_reconciliation_note.resolution: This file defines a two-tier pipeline: macro_generation_order (region sequencing, already established, referenced not restated) and micro_layer_pipeline (content-type sequencing within each macro-ordered region). This mirrors standard AAA open-world procedural pipelines, which sequence macro region placement first and layer content types within each placed region second.

macro_generation_order.note: Full detail (roles, depends_on) lives in Meridian_Master.json and DistrictRegistry.json — not duplicated here.

micro_layer_pipeline.action: Load Meridian_Master.json as the single manifest; resolve all registry_references.
Run all pre_generation_checks before any content is generated.
Generate road_hierarchy geometry per macro-ordered region.
Generate generic architectural shell geometry per building_layer category — typology kits (e.g. White Zones Community Trust Center) before bespoke one-off structures.
Layer district-specific bespoke sub-location detail onto the generic shells from step 4, per macro-ordered region — landmarks, named NPC hubs, mission-critical rooms.
Generate Live Network and Dead Network lines, stations, and interchanges. Never merge the two networks.
Apply power, water/climate, and maintenance service tiers per district.
Apply materials, color language, and environmental storytelling assets per district.
micro_layer_pipeline.cross_reference: environment_layer; each district's own Materials / Color Language / Environmental Storytelling sections

micro_layer_pipeline.action: Place mission-critical content, boss encounters, and side-quest triggers.
micro_layer_pipeline.hard_constraint: olympus_spire_server_hub combat content is NOT generated at this step — see error_handling.highest_priority_flag below.

micro_layer_pipeline.action: Build pedestrian, vehicle, metro, underground, and rooftop traversal graphs.
Apply the five-district lighting spectrum. Sector 0 additionally requires its four-state dynamic lighting system, the one district with story-tied baseline lighting change.
Assign generated content to streaming cells and set cell_dependencies.
Apply all 30 district gating layers plus the 1 cross-district layer, and the 8 layer_type_categories.
Terminal state. No further automated steps follow — human review begins here.

module_dependencies.reconciliation_status: The two discrepancies flagged during NavigationGraph.json and GameplayGraph.json generation (dependence on then-nonexistent infrastructure_graph and data_layers) are now closed — both files exist as of this package's current state.
module_dependencies.note: Full dependency map lives in Meridian_Master.json — not duplicated here.

execution_order.description: For each region in macro_generation_order, execute micro_layer_pipeline steps 3 through 13 before proceeding to the next region. Steps 1, 2, 14, and 15 run once globally, not per-region.
execution_order.parallelism: Per Meridian_Master.json generation_settings — parallel generation permitted within a single macro step, not across macro steps.

highest_priority_flag.rule: HALT generation of olympus_spire_server_hub combat-encounter content specifically. Generate the space's architecture (per Olympus_Spire.md Sec. 14, 36) normally — this flag blocks encounter design only, not the room's existence.

moderate_priority_flag.rule: Generate metro_archives reset-mechanic architecture and the three reset tiers normally. Flag for a technical feasibility review pass before final polish, not before greybox.

low_priority_flags.rule: Generate normally without interruption. Log a sign-off reminder for the writing team; do not block any pipeline step.

missing_reference_error.rule: If any registry_reference in Meridian_Master.json fails to resolve to an existing file, HALT the full pipeline at step 1 (import). Do not proceed to validation with a partial manifest.

cross_file_inconsistency_error.rule: If any cross-reference check (as performed manually during this package's own construction — e.g. route IDs, tunnel IDs, sub-location IDs) fails at generation time, HALT the current macro region step and report the specific mismatched IDs. Do not silently drop or auto-correct mismatched references.

recovery_rules.regeneration_policy: Locked packages (the five approved districts, Meridian_Master_Plan.md) are never regenerated, only referenced — consistent with Meridian_Master.json builder_configuration.
recovery_rules.partial_failure_recovery: On failure mid-region, resume from the last successfully completed micro_layer_pipeline step within that region. Do not restart already-completed macro regions.
recovery_rules.manual_override: Any HALT triggered by error_handling above requires explicit human sign-off to resume — the Builder does not auto-resume past a flagged discrepancy or missing reference.

validation_rules.authority: Extends Meridian_Master.json validation_rules with the two additional checks specific to this package's own construction history (cross-file ID resolution, server hub gating).

future_expansion_rules.additional_note: Any Phase 2 (connective tissue) or Phase 3 (new district) work extending this package must add a corresponding entry to DistrictRegistry.json, RoadNetwork.json, and WorldPartition.json at minimum before BuilderRules.json's pipeline is re-run — partial registration in only one file is not sufficient for the Builder to generate new content correctly.
